info.name: MailCue API
info.description: Realistic email testing server REST API

request.description:
Authenticate with username + password.

Returns an access token in the response body and sets the refresh
token as an ``httpOnly`` cookie for the web UI.

If the user has TOTP enabled, returns a ``LoginStepResponse`` with
a short-lived temp token instead; the client must call ``/login/2fa``.
Complete login by verifying a TOTP code after step 1.
Create a new user account. **Admin only.**
Change the current user's password.
Reset a user's password without knowing the old one. **Admin only.**

Generate a TOTP secret and QR code for authenticator setup.

The secret is stored encrypted but TOTP is **not yet enabled** until
the user confirms with a valid code via ``POST /totp/confirm``.
Verify a TOTP code from the authenticator app and enable 2FA.
Disable TOTP 2FA. Requires the current password and a valid TOTP code.
Return the currently authenticated user's profile.
Clear the httpOnly refresh_token cookie.

Exchange a refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.

The refresh token can be passed in the JSON body **or** read from
the ``refresh_token`` httpOnly cookie.
List every permission scope an API key can be granted.
List all API keys for the current user (without raw key values).

Generate a new API key for the current user.

The raw key value is returned **only once** in this response. A key
created via another API key cannot grant scopes or mailboxes broader
than the key that created it (no privilege escalation).

Update an existing API key's name and/or permissions in place.

The key's secret value is **not** regenerated, so integrations already
using it keep working under the new permissions -- the intended path for
tightening legacy full-access keys. Only fields present in the request
body are changed.
Revoke an API key by removing it permanently.
List all users. **Admin only.**
Update a user's admin-editable fields. **Admin only.**

Deactivate a user account. **Admin only.**

The user's mailboxes are preserved but become orphaned.

query.description: Group conversations: sort the page by (thread_id, date asc).

request.description:
List emails in a mailbox with pagination, search, and sorting.

Emails are fetched directly from IMAP. The ``search`` parameter
maps to IMAP ``TEXT`` search which covers subject and body.
Fetch a single email by its IMAP UID with full body and headers.
Delete an email by UID (sets \Deleted flag and expunges).
Download the raw RFC 5322 source of an email as a ``.eml`` file.
Download a specific MIME attachment identified by its part ID.

Send an email via the local SMTP server (Postfix).

The sender address (``from_address``) must belong to the
authenticated user's mailbox (admins may send from any).

Inject an email directly into a mailbox via IMAP APPEND.

**Admin only.** Bypasses SMTP delivery entirely -- the email
appears in the target mailbox immediately. Ideal for test data setup.

Inject multiple emails into mailboxes in a single request.

**Admin only.** Each email in the ``emails`` array is injected
independently. Partial failures are reported in the response
without aborting the entire batch.
Validate email address format, DNS domain MX/NS, SMTP availability, and disposable status.

List active mailboxes with email/unread counts.

Users only see their own mailboxes; an API key further sees only the
mailboxes within its allow-list.

Create a new mailbox and provision it on the mail server.

Any authenticated user can create mailboxes up to their quota.

Delete (deactivate) a mailbox by address and remove it from Dovecot.

Admins can delete any mailbox; users can delete their own.
The Maildir data is preserved by default.

Retrieve email counts and folder statistics via IMAP STATUS.

Falls back to zeroed stats when the mail server is unreachable
(common in local development without Docker).

Delete all emails from a mailbox across all folders.

Admins can purge any mailbox; users can purge their own.

query.description: When true, sort the page by (thread_id, date asc) so conversations render as contiguous groups.

request.description: List emails for a specific mailbox (path-based variant).
Fetch a single email by UID from a specific mailbox.
Delete an email by UID from a specific mailbox.
Delete multiple emails by UID from a specific mailbox.
Toggle read/unread status on an email by setting or clearing the \Seen flag.
Mark an email as spam by moving it from the source folder to Junk.
Mark an email as not spam by moving it from Junk back to INBOX.

Set or update the display name for a mailbox.

The user must own the mailbox (or be an admin).

Set or update the email signature for a mailbox.

The user must own the mailbox (or be an admin).

Open an SSE stream for the authenticated user.

Events are broadcast from the in-process event bus.  The stream
sends a heartbeat comment every 30 seconds to keep the TCP
connection alive through proxies and load balancers.

Generate a new GPG keypair for a mailbox address.

The user must own the target mailbox (admins may target any).
List GPG keys for the current user's mailboxes only.
Retrieve a GPG key by mailbox address.
Delete all GPG keys for a mailbox address.
Export the ASCII-armored public key for a mailbox address.
Download the raw armored PGP public key as a ``.asc`` file.
Publish a GPG public key to keys.openpgp.org.

Import an armored PGP public key.

If ``mailbox_address`` is specified, the user must own that mailbox.
List all managed email domains. **Admin only.**
Add a new email domain and generate DKIM keys. **Admin only.**
Get domain details including required DNS records. **Admin only.**
Remove a managed domain and clean up config. **Admin only.**

Serve the MTA-STS policy file (RFC 8461).

This must be accessible at https://mta-sts.{domain}/.well-known/mta-sts.txt
for each managed domain.
Run live DNS checks for a domain. **Admin only.**

Refresh DNS lookups + return per-record expected/published/drift state.

Read-only: never flips the canonical ``*_verified`` booleans (use
``POST /verify-dns`` for that).  Designed to be cheap and pollable from
the admin UI every 60s.  **Admin only.**
Serve MTA-STS policy at the RFC-mandated path.
Return metadata for both the server and CA certificates. **Admin only.**
Download the CA certificate as a PEM file. **Admin only.**
Return server-wide settings. **Admin only.**
Update server-wide settings. **Admin only.**
Return custom TLS certificate metadata. **Admin only.** No PEM content.
Upload a custom TLS certificate + private key. **Admin only.**
Return the current production readiness status. **Admin only.**
List all forwarding rules for the authenticated user.

Create a new forwarding rule.

The rule begins matching immediately when ``enabled`` is ``True``
(the default).
Fetch a single forwarding rule by ID.

Update an existing forwarding rule (partial update).

Only fields present in the request body are modified.
Permanently delete a forwarding rule.

Dry-run test a forwarding rule against sample email data.

No action is actually executed -- the response indicates whether
the rule would have matched and which patterns contributed.
List all email aliases. **Admin only.**
Create a new email alias. **Admin only.**
Get a single alias by ID. **Admin only.**
Update an existing alias. **Admin only.**
Delete an alias. **Admin only.**
Return the stored Mailcue tunnel-client public key. **Admin only.**

Force ``tunnels.json`` to be rewritten right now. **Admin only.**

Useful when the sidecar's config volume becomes available after the
API has already started -- e.g. running this immediately after the
sidecar is deployed avoids waiting for the next CRUD operation.
List all configured tunnels. **Admin only.**
Create a new tunnel. **Admin only.**
Fetch a single tunnel by ID. **Admin only.**
Apply a partial update to a tunnel. **Admin only.**
Permanently delete a tunnel. **Admin only.**

Probe a tunnel edge with a 5-second TCP connect. **Admin only.**

The actual Noise IK handshake is the sidecar's responsibility -- this
only confirms the edge port is reachable from the API host.
Lightweight health probe for Docker HEALTHCHECK and load balancers.
Dynamically generated OpenClaw SKILL.md for this MailCue instance.
